questions.text: A naturally occurring substance that has a definite chemical composition is a ________.
questions.explanation: Minerals are distributed unevenly and formed in different types of geological environments.

questions.text: Minerals that contain metals in raw form are called:
questions.explanation: Iron ore, bauxite, and manganese are examples of metallic minerals.

questions.text: Metallic minerals that contain iron are called ________ minerals.
questions.explanation: Ferrous comes from the Latin word for iron.

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-metallic mineral?
questions.explanation: Limestone, mica, and gypsum do not contain metals.

questions.text: The process of taking out minerals from rocks buried under the earth's surface is called:
questions.explanation: Mining can be open-cast or shaft mining.

questions.text: Minerals that lie at shallow depths and are taken out by removing the surface layer are known as ________ mining.
questions.explanation: Open-cast mining is used for minerals near the surface.

questions.text: Deep bores made to reach mineral deposits at great depths are called:
questions.explanation: Shaft mining is used for minerals found at great depths.

questions.text: The process of boring deep wells to take out petroleum and natural gas is called:
questions.explanation: Drilling is common for liquid or gaseous resources found far below ground.

questions.text: Minerals that lie near the surface and are simply dug out are called:
questions.explanation: Quarrying is used for building materials like stone or sand.

questions.text: Which continent is the leading producer of iron ore in the world?
questions.explanation: Europe has vast deposits of iron ore in Russia, Ukraine, and Sweden.

questions.text: Asia produces more than half of the world's ________.
questions.explanation: China, Malaysia, and Indonesia are among the world's leading tin producers.

questions.text: Which country in South America is the leading producer of copper?
questions.explanation: Chile and Peru are leading copper producers.

questions.text: A rare diamond which is found in nature is ________ in color.
questions.explanation: Green diamonds are the rarest.

questions.text: Power resources are broadly categorized as ________.
questions.explanation: Conventional resources have been in use for a long time, while non-conventional are newer.

questions.text: Which of the following is a conventional source of energy?
questions.explanation: Firewood and fossil fuels are the two main conventional energy sources.

questions.text: Coal is also known as ________.
questions.explanation: Coal was formed millions of years ago from giant ferns and swamps buried under Earth layers.

questions.text: Petroleum and its derivatives are called ________ because they are very valuable.
questions.explanation: Petroleum is used in a vast range of products.

questions.text: Which gas is found with petroleum deposits and is released when crude oil is brought to the surface?
questions.explanation: Natural gas is used as domestic and industrial fuel.

questions.text: The electricity generated from water is called ________.
questions.explanation: Falling water turns turbines to produce electricity.

questions.text: Which of these is a non-conventional source of energy?
questions.explanation: Non-conventional sources are renewable and generally eco-friendly.

questions.text: Energy obtained from the nucleus of atoms of naturally occurring radioactive elements like Uranium and Thorium is:
questions.explanation: Nuclear reactors use nuclear fission to produce power.

questions.text: Heat energy obtained from the interior of the earth is called ________ energy.
questions.explanation: Temperature rises as we go deeper into the earth, providing heat.

questions.text: Energy generated from tides is called ________ energy.
questions.explanation: Dams are built at narrow openings of the sea to harness tidal energy.

questions.text: Organic waste like dead plant and animal material transformed by bacteria into gaseous fuel is called ________.
questions.explanation: Biogas is an excellent fuel for cooking and lighting.

questions.text: The Kolar gold mines are located in ________.
questions.explanation: The Kolar mines in Karnataka are among the deepest in the world.

questions.text: Which of these is used in the electrical and electronic industries because of its insulating properties?
questions.explanation: Mica can withstand high voltage and has low power loss factor.

questions.text: Bauxite is the ore from which ________ is obtained.
questions.explanation: Aluminum is used in aircraft and automobiles due to its light weight.

questions.text: Where is the largest solar plant of India located?
questions.explanation: The Kamuthi solar power project is in Tamil Nadu.

questions.text: Manikaran in Himachal Pradesh is famous for which type of energy?
questions.explanation: Manikaran is known for its hot springs which provide geothermal energy.

questions.text: Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu is famous for ________ power.
questions.explanation: It is home to the Madras Atomic Power Station.

questions.text: Which country has the world's largest geothermal power plants?
questions.explanation: USA has the highest capacity, followed by New Zealand and Iceland.

questions.text: The first country in the world to develop hydroelectricity was ________.
questions.explanation: Norway was the pioneer in hydroelectric power.

questions.text: Minerals are ________ resources.
questions.explanation: Minerals take thousands of years to form and are exhausted by human use.

questions.text: Recycling of metals is a way to ________ minerals.
questions.explanation: Using scrap metal reduces the need for mining new ores.

questions.text: Which of these is a ferrous mineral?
questions.explanation: Manganese is used in the manufacture of steel.

questions.text: Most minerals are found in which type of rocks?
questions.explanation: Metallic minerals like iron and copper are usually found in igneous and metamorphic rock formations.

questions.text: The Gulf of Kachchh in India is a good site for ________ energy.
questions.explanation: The large tidal range makes it suitable for tidal power.

questions.text: Which of these is a leading producer of natural gas in the world?
questions.explanation: Russia and Norway have huge natural gas reserves.

questions.text: Petroleum is found between the layers of ________.
questions.explanation: It is trapped between layers of sedimentary rocks.

questions.text: CNG stands for ________.
questions.explanation: CNG is a cleaner alternative to petrol and diesel.
